title: Resource Graphs, Cache Evidence, Budgets, and Performance Non-Claims · Runtime Contract: Stakeholder Budget
summary: The tool reports document bytes as total page weight, ignores encoded versus decoded sizes and browser discovery, and turns one server timing into user performance. Predict owner authority, URL and HTTP state, Go ownership, bounded work, accessibility impact, failure, cleanup, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the crawler go stakeholder budget increment in static resource and delivery evidence dossier. Learners progress through prediction, explic…
objectives: Set page-class count, byte, third-party, image, font, script, and uncertainty budgets with owners and exceptions.
Report cache fields, validators, variation, redirects, compression, and cold or warm assumptions without simulating browser cache blindly.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

Real DNS, sockets, x/net/html, goquery, Colly, files, processes, race, load, assistive-technology review, containers, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Set page-class count, byte, third-party, image, font, script, and uncertainty budgets with owners and exceptions.

For Authorized site auditing and Go Defend the Stakeholder Budget model · Theory Stakeholder Budget, challenge the documented misconception with runtime, failure, race, or toolchain evidence: One universal page-weight budget fits every public service.
